title: Apply: embryology and evolution
description: Apply your understanding of embryology and evolution in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mice develop as embryos inside their mother and undergo live birth. The following illustration shows an adult mouse.

[[☃ image 1]]

The next three illustrations show mouse embryos. Each one is in a different stage of development. The illustrations are not drawn to scale.

**Of the three embryos, which one is in the EARLIEST stage of development?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: As an embryo develops, it takes on more of the specialized features of its species.
So, the embryo in the earliest stage of development will have the *fewest* features in common with an adult mouse.

Of the three embryos shown, the one with the fewest features in common with an adult mouse is:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A mouse with white fur. The mouse has a head, eyes, a tail, four limbs, fingers, toes, ears, a snout, and whiskers.

choices.content: ![A mouse embryo with a spinal cord.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/28ea4510333a44daf3bc3bdc11f5245f.png)
![A mouse embryo with a spinal cord, a head, eyes, a tail, and limbs.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f01d60f07aa019bbad0d9ad40bcc909f.png)
![A mouse embryo with a spinal cord, a head, eyes, a tail, limbs, fingers, toes, ears, and a snout.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d121aa52591dc9e230884f9fba838bf2.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Mice develop as embryos inside their mother and undergo live birth. The following illustration shows an adult mouse.

[[☃ image 1]]

The next three illustrations show mouse embryos. Each one is in a different stage of development. The illustrations are not drawn to scale.

**Of the three embryos, which one is in the LATEST stage of development?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: As an embryo develops, it takes on more of the specialized features of its species.
So, the embryo in the latest stage of development will have the *most* features in common with an adult mouse.

Of the three embryos shown, the one with the most features in common with an adult mouse is:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A mouse with white fur. The mouse has a head, eyes, a tail, four limbs, fingers, toes, ears, a snout, and whiskers.

choices.content: ![A mouse embryo with an embryonic spinal cord, a head, eyes, a tail, limbs, fingers, toes, ears, and a snout.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d121aa52591dc9e230884f9fba838bf2.png)
![A mouse embryo with an embryonic spinal cord, a head, eyes, a tail, and limbs.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f01d60f07aa019bbad0d9ad40bcc909f.png)
![A mouse embryo with an embryonic spinal cord.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/28ea4510333a44daf3bc3bdc11f5245f.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Zebrafish develop as embryos inside of eggs. The following illustration shows an adult zebrafish.

[[☃ image 1]]

The next three illustrations show zebrafish embryos. Each one is in a different stage of development. The illustrations are not drawn to scale.

**Of the three embryos, which one is in the LATEST stage of development?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: As an embryo develops, it takes on more of the specialized features of its species.
So, the embryo in the latest stage of development will have the *most* features in common with an adult zebrafish.

Of the three embryos shown, the one with the most features in common with an adult zebrafish is:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A zebrafish with fins, eyes, gills, and stripes.

choices.content: ![A zebrafish embryo with an embryonic spinal cord, an elongated body, eyes, fin-like structures, and embryonic gills.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a7ef23e1a44576c14642afc3a5191030.png)
![A zebrafish embryo with an embryonic spinal cord, an elongated body, and eyes.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7466d550de3b6bf04f9828c0e37799ed.png)
![A zebrafish embryo with an embryonic spinal cord.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3a33f897664c474ddd5d2011f5e2265e.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Pharyngeal arches are found in the embryos of many species. The drawing below shows a human embryo and its pharyngeal arches.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which statement about pharyngeal arches best supports the claim that all vertebrates are related through evolution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: An illustration of a human embryo. Bulges in the embryo’s neck area are labeled pharyngeal arches.

choices.content: Pharyngeal arches are found in the embryos of all vertebrates.
Some vertebrate embryos have pharyngeal arches, and others do not.

assessment_items.question_content:
Forelimb buds are found in the embryos of many species. The drawing below shows a human embryo with a labeled forelimb bud.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which statement about forelimb buds best supports the claim that humans, bats, and birds are related through evolution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: An illustration of a human embryo. A bulge on the upper part of the embryo’s body is labeled forelimb bud.

choices.content: Forelimb buds are found in the embryos of humans, bats, and birds.
Human and bat embryos have forelimb buds, but bird embryos do not.

assessment_items.question_content:
Hindlimb buds are found in the embryos of many species. The drawing below shows a human embryo with a labeled hindlimb bud.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which statement about hindlimb buds best supports the claim that humans, elephants, and whales are related through evolution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: An illustration of a human embryo. A bulge on the lower part of the embryo’s body is labeled hindlimb bud.

choices.content: Hindlimb buds are found in the embryos of humans, elephants, and whales.
Human and elephant embryos have hindlimb buds, but whale embryos do not.

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the following statements. Then, answer the question below.

- Human embryos have structures called notochords. The notochord develops into part of the backbone as the embryo grows.
- Lamprey embryos also have notochords. In lampreys, notochords are found in adults, too. 
- Sea stars do not have notochords at any stage of development. 

**Based on this information, which evolutionary tree correctly shows the relationship between humans, lampreys, and sea stars?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Embryos of different species can have similarities that are not visible when the organisms are fully formed. These similarities can be evidence of evolutionary relationships.

The text tells us that both human and lamprey embryos have notochords. The text also tells us that sea stars do not have notochords. So, the correct evolutionary tree will show that humans and lampreys are more closely related to each other than they are to sea stars.

In other words, the correct evolutionary tree will show that humans and lampreys share a common ancestor in the more recent past, and that humans, lampreys, and sea stars share a common ancestor in the more distant past.

The correct answer is:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled human at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled lamprey. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled sea star.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9d22f70d090b8f12812cb09ba7e92c55.png)

![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled sea star at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled human. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled lamprey.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5867c9d5856719ca3835702ef8d65dfc.png)

![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled lamprey at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled sea star. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled human.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2212a1520f1eaeea9e0cd2c6585bf0aa.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the following statements. Then, answer the question below.

- Toad embryos have structures called notochords. The notochord develops into part of the backbone as the embryo grows.
- Lancelet embryos also have notochords. In lancelets, notochords are found in adults, too. 
- Urchins do not have notochords at any stage of development. 

**Based on this information, which evolutionary tree correctly shows the relationship between toads, lancelets, and urchins?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Embryos of different species can have similarities that are not visible when the organisms are fully formed. These similarities can be evidence of evolutionary relationships.

The text tells us that both toad and lancelet embryos have notochords. The text also tells us that urchins do not have notochords. So, the correct evolutionary tree will show that toads and lancelets are more closely related to each other than they are to urchins.

In other words, the correct evolutionary tree will show that toads and lancelets share a common ancestor in the more recent past, and that toads, lancelets, and urchins share a common ancestor in the more distant past.

The correct answer is:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled toad at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled lancelet. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled urchin.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9b69a18a767117e36927d175ef29b6e1.png)

![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled urchin at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled toad. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled lancelet.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a2098d9f8062d4415b70053f5a63a149.png)

![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled lancelet at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled urchin. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled toad.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/95a5b62e93734ee37b5d7561126cf845.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the following statements. Then, answer the question below.

- Clownfish embryos have structures called notochords. The notochord develops into part of the backbone as the embryo grows.
- Sea squirt embryos also have notochords. 
- Octopuses do not have notochords at any stage of development. 

**Based on this information, which evolutionary tree correctly shows the relationship between clownfish, sea squirts, and octopuses?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]
hints: Embryos of different species can have similarities that are not visible when the organisms are fully formed. These similarities can be evidence of evolutionary relationships.

The text tells us that both clownfish and sea squirt embryos have notochords. The text also tells us that octopuses do not have notochords. So, the correct evolutionary tree will show that clownfish and sea squirts are more closely related to each other than they are to octopuses.

In other words, the correct evolutionary tree will show that clownfish and sea squirts share a common ancestor in the more recent past, and that clownfish, sea squirts, and octopuses share a common ancestor in the more distant past.

The correct answer is:

[[☃ image 1]]

choices.content: ![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled clownfish at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled sea squirt. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled octopus.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/45578093781d6a5c22408f7b14b002a6.png)

![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled octopus at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled clownfish. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled sea squirt.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/887cb47857ea8a5ea3fdd2f0d53fc97a.png)

![A diagonal line starts at the bottom of a diagram and travels up and to the right. It is labeled sea squirt at the top. Two diagonal lines branch off of this main line and travel up and to the left until they reach the same level as the top of the main line. The shorter of these lines branches off near the top and is labeled octopus. A slightly longer line branches off near the bottom and is labeled clownfish.](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/af59b70ad488d3d0b32d29fd00d26719.png)

assessment_items.question_content:
The illustration below shows a chicken embryo and adult. The illustration is not drawn to scale.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which statement correctly describes a difference between the embryo and the adult chicken?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A drawing of a chicken embryo and an adult chicken. The embryo has eyes that are very large for the size of its head. The embryo has wings, legs, feet with toes, a tail, and an embryonic spinal cord. The adult has small eyes and a beak, feathers, a tail, wings, legs, and feet with toes.

choices.content: The embryo has large eyes compared to the size of its head, but the adult does not.
The embryo has legs with feet and toes, but the adult does not.
The adult has forelimbs that are wings, but the embryo does not.

assessment_items.question_content:
The illustration below shows an 11-day-old mouse embryo and an adult mouse. The illustration is not drawn to scale.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which feature can be found in both the embryo and the adult mouse?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A drawing of a mouse embryo and an adult mouse. The embryo has a head, a tail, and an embryonic spinal cord. The adult has eyes, whiskers, a tail, four limbs, ears, and fur.

choices.content: a tail
whiskers
ears

assessment_items.question_content:
The illustration below shows a human embryo and a child. The illustration is not drawn to scale.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which feature can be found in both the human embryo and the child?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.alt: A drawing of a human embryo and a child. The embryo has a head, a tail, and an embryonic spinal cord. The child has a head with eyes, ears, a nose, and a mouth, two legs, and two hands, each with five fingers.

choices.content: a head
hands with fingers
ears
